{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_b32a7919a62a45e2817362c4","type":"tool_result","content":"290\tThe voice model's system prompt controls narration style, batching behavior, urgency handling, and multi-turn topic tracking. Read per session-turn from a file (hot-reload).\n291\t\n292\tIn audio mode there is no canvas. The voice model reads `result.summary` directly. When a user transitions from audio to canvas, pending results are rendered using the component library and appear on canvas — session continuity means the canvas catches up.\n293\t\n294\t### 7. UI-Regen Agent\n295\t\n296\t**Runtime:** Claude Code via NEEDLE (task bead)\n297\t\n298\tSteward of the component library — asynchronous only. It is never on the hot path and never renders a card a user is waiting on.\n299\t\n300\t**Hot-path selection and rendering happen in the server, not here.** On every dispatch the server picks the component deterministically — highest `match_score` in `component_usage_patterns` for the result's `result_type`, no LLM call (lookup result_types embed the router's `lookup_kind`, so `lookup:logs:ibkr-mcp` and `lookup:config:ibkr-mcp` are distinct keys selecting distinct components) — fills its template with the result data per layout bucket, writes the output to `card_cache`, and streams the rendered HTML over SSE; the client injects it. When nothing matches (a first-ever result shape, or no score above threshold), the server flags the result and the client renders it with the built-in generic fallback card (see Component Library) — a novel shape never blanks the canvas, it just renders plainly until the library catches up.\n301\t\n302\t**Escaping contract (render path).** Template fill is the escaping boundary: every interpolated value — fetch output, `result.data` fields, `summary`, raw log lines — is HTML-escaped at template-fill time, and templates receive data as text only, never raw HTML from results, so a markup-looking log line renders as literal text instead of breaking layout or executing in the canvas. This binds LLM-generated component templates and the built-in generic fallback card's key/value grid alike (the fallback escapes client-side, where it renders). The same contract binds every client-filled built-in card: SSE-event values interpolated into built-in templates on the client — per-source progress states, bead refs, elapsed-time strings, error details, and above all worker/LLM-authored free text like the stuck card's refusal reasons lifted from bead comments — are inserted as text nodes (or escaped identically to the fallback card's values), never as HTML.\n303\t\n304\tThe UI-regen agent's job is making the library catch up. Given a result shape that fell to the fallback card or a weak match:\n305\t1. Finds the best-fit existing component (semantic match against component descriptions) and records the mapping in `component_usage_patterns` so the hot-path lookup hits it next time\n306\t2. If no good fit, generates a new component from scratch and stores it\n307\t3. On feedback, iterates the component (updates library, triggers canvas re-render)\n308\t\n309\tMulti-step: reads library, generates or selects, writes back. Claude Code's file access and tool use make this the right runtime.\n310\t\n311\tCreated as a task bead; not on the hot path. Canvas updates in place when a component is versioned (SSE push: `component_updated: {component_id, version}`).\n312\t\n313\t### 8. Self-Modification Agent\n314\t\n315\t**Runtime:** Claude Code via NEEDLE (task bead)\n316\t\n317\tReads and writes the artifacts that encode system behavior:\n318\t- Router prompt (`prompts/router.md`)\n319\t- Project registry (`config/registry.yaml`)\n320\t- Strand prompts (`prompts/fetch/*.md` — one per intent type, per `lookup_kind` for lookup; `prompts/synthesize.md`; `prompts/escalate/task-profile.md`)\n321\t- Urgency classifier prompt (`prompts/urgency.md`)\n322\t- Voice model prompt (`prompts/voice.md`)\n323\t- Monitoring config (`config/monitoring.yaml`)\n324\t- Exception routing rules (`config/exceptions.yaml`)\n325\t\n326\tWorkflow for every modification:\n327\t1. Read current artifact\n328\t2. Understand intent of change\n329\t3. Generate updated artifact\n330\t4. Surface diff to user (in canvas or narrated in audio mode)\n331\t5. On approval: write artifact, hot-reload takes effect on next invocation\n332\t6. On rejection: discard\n333\t\n334\tSafety model:\n335\t- Diffs before every application — no silent changes\n336\t- Versioning is git: every artifact write (`prompts/*.md`, `config/*.yaml`) is a git commit with a machine-generated message; rolling back any update is one instruction → `git revert` of that commit. Component library artifacts get the same guarantee through the `component_versions` table. No bespoke version store.\n337\t- Confidence threshold: unambiguous changes (adding an alias) can auto-apply with notification; structural changes always require explicit approval\n338\t- Out-of-band kill switch: self-modification can break the very interface used to undo it (a bad router prompt means no more routed instructions). Two escape hatches bypass the interface entirely: `ADC_SELFMOD_FREEZE=1` in the server environment, or `adc freeze` from the CLI (which falls back to touching a `data/FREEZE` sentinel file if the server is unresponsive). While frozen, every self-modification and auto-apply write is refused, and `adc restore-artifacts` git-reverts `prompts/` and `config/` to the last-known-good commit — no canvas, voice session, or router involvement required. **Break-glass caveat:** the sentinel-file fallback and the git revert are local filesystem/git operations against the server's artifact store — `adc` is a thin HTTP client (see adc CLI connectivity), so from a remote machine neither works exactly when the server is unresponsive. The break-glass procedure is: SSH into the server host first, then run `adc freeze` / `adc restore-artifacts` there.\n339\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"83fa3851-a0e5-4045-8a42-fd243d18effa","uuid":"864e23ab-cf12-424e-9ef1-4eb7fa878621","timestamp":"2026-07-23T14:28:13.693Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/aide-de-camp/docs/plan/plan.md","content":"The voice model's system prompt controls narration style, batching behavior, urgency handling, and multi-turn topic tracking. Read per session-turn from a file (hot-reload).\n\nIn audio mode there is no canvas. The voice model reads `result.summary` directly. When a user transitions from audio to canvas, pending results are rendered using the component library and appear on canvas — session continuity means the canvas catches up.\n\n### 7. UI-Regen Agent\n\n**Runtime:** Claude Code via NEEDLE (task bead)\n\nSteward of the component library — asynchronous only. It is never on the hot path and never renders a card a user is waiting on.\n\n**Hot-path selection and rendering happen in the server, not here.** On every dispatch the server picks the component deterministically — highest `match_score` in `component_usage_patterns` for the result's `result_type`, no LLM call (lookup result_types embed the router's `lookup_kind`, so `lookup:logs:ibkr-mcp` and `lookup:config:ibkr-mcp` are distinct keys selecting distinct components) — fills its template with the result data per layout bucket, writes the output to `card_cache`, and streams the rendered HTML over SSE; the client injects it. When nothing matches (a first-ever result shape, or no score above threshold), the server flags the result and the client renders it with the built-in generic fallback card (see Component Library) — a novel shape never blanks the canvas, it just renders plainly until the library catches up.\n\n**Escaping contract (render path).** Template fill is the escaping boundary: every interpolated value — fetch output, `result.data` fields, `summary`, raw log lines — is HTML-escaped at template-fill time, and templates receive data as text only, never raw HTML from results, so a markup-looking log line renders as literal text instead of breaking layout or executing in the canvas. This binds LLM-generated component templates and the built-in generic fallback card's key/value grid alike (the fallback escapes client-side, where it renders). The same contract binds every client-filled built-in card: SSE-event values interpolated into built-in templates on the client — per-source progress states, bead refs, elapsed-time strings, error details, and above all worker/LLM-authored free text like the stuck card's refusal reasons lifted from bead comments — are inserted as text nodes (or escaped identically to the fallback card's values), never as HTML.\n\nThe UI-regen agent's job is making the library catch up. Given a result shape that fell to the fallback card or a weak match:\n1. Finds the best-fit existing component (semantic match against component descriptions) and records the mapping in `component_usage_patterns` so the hot-path lookup hits it next time\n2. If no good fit, generates a new component from scratch and stores it\n3. On feedback, iterates the component (updates library, triggers canvas re-render)\n\nMulti-step: reads library, generates or selects, writes back. Claude Code's file access and tool use make this the right runtime.\n\nCreated as a task bead; not on the hot path. Canvas updates in place when a component is versioned (SSE push: `component_updated: {component_id, version}`).\n\n### 8. Self-Modification Agent\n\n**Runtime:** Claude Code via NEEDLE (task bead)\n\nReads and writes the artifacts that encode system behavior:\n- Router prompt (`prompts/router.md`)\n- Project registry (`config/registry.yaml`)\n- Strand prompts (`prompts/fetch/*.md` — one per intent type, per `lookup_kind` for lookup; `prompts/synthesize.md`; `prompts/escalate/task-profile.md`)\n- Urgency classifier prompt (`prompts/urgency.md`)\n- Voice model prompt (`prompts/voice.md`)\n- Monitoring config (`config/monitoring.yaml`)\n- Exception routing rules (`config/exceptions.yaml`)\n\nWorkflow for every modification:\n1. Read current artifact\n2. Understand intent of change\n3. Generate updated artifact\n4. Surface diff to user (in canvas or narrated in audio mode)\n5. On approval: write artifact, hot-reload takes effect on next invocation\n6. On rejection: discard\n\nSafety model:\n- Diffs before every application — no silent changes\n- Versioning is git: every artifact write (`prompts/*.md`, `config/*.yaml`) is a git commit with a machine-generated message; rolling back any update is one instruction → `git revert` of that commit. Component library artifacts get the same guarantee through the `component_versions` table. No bespoke version store.\n- Confidence threshold: unambiguous changes (adding an alias) can auto-apply with notification; structural changes always require explicit approval\n- Out-of-band kill switch: self-modification can break the very interface used to undo it (a bad router prompt means no more routed instructions). Two escape hatches bypass the interface entirely: `ADC_SELFMOD_FREEZE=1` in the server environment, or `adc freeze` from the CLI (which falls back to touching a `data/FREEZE` sentinel file if the server is unresponsive). While frozen, every self-modification and auto-apply write is refused, and `adc restore-artifacts` git-reverts `prompts/` and `config/` to the last-known-good commit — no canvas, voice session, or router involvement required. **Break-glass caveat:** the sentinel-file fallback and the git revert are local filesystem/git operations against the server's artifact store — `adc` is a thin HTTP client (see adc CLI connectivity), so from a remote machine neither works exactly when the server is unresponsive. The break-glass procedure is: SSH into the server host first, then run `adc freeze` / `adc restore-artifacts` there.\n","numLines":50,"startLine":290,"totalLines":1203}}}

Consequence for Phase 5: built-in cards across all four families are exempt from — and never seeded for — the component-library requirements in the seeding runbook and rehearsal checklist.\n595\t\n596\t---\n597\t\n598\t## File System Layout\n599\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"83fa3851-a0e5-4045-8a42-fd243d18effa","uuid":"15752034-6c45-4ead-ad6a-61e62b4f2e71","timestamp":"2026-07-23T14:28:13.698Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/aide-de-camp/docs/plan/plan.md","content":")\n\ncomponent_versions (\n  component_id  TEXT,\n  version       INTEGER,\n  html_template TEXT,\n  created_at    INTEGER,\n  change_note   TEXT,\n  PRIMARY KEY (component_id, version)\n)\n\ncard_cache (\n  result_id         TEXT,\n  component_id      TEXT,\n  component_version INTEGER,\n  layout_bucket     TEXT,  -- 'compact' | 'normal' | 'expanded'\n  rendered_html     TEXT,\n  created_at        INTEGER,\n  PRIMARY KEY (result_id, component_id, layout_bucket)\n)\n\ncomponent_tags (\n  component_id TEXT,\n  tag TEXT,\n  PRIMARY KEY (component_id, tag)\n)\n\ncomponent_usage_patterns (\n  component_id TEXT,\n  result_type TEXT,  -- thread-level key matching results.result_type, e.g.\n                     -- \"status:options-pipeline\", \"lookup:logs:ibkr-mcp\",\n                     -- \"lookup:config:ibkr-mcp\" (lookup keys carry lookup_kind,\n                     -- so log-lookup and config-lookup components select\n                     -- independently) — never per-source (\"pod-status\")\n                     -- granularity; a thread's sources aggregate into one card\n  match_score REAL,  -- 0-1\n  sample_count INTEGER,\n  last_matched INTEGER,\n  PRIMARY KEY (component_id, result_type)\n)\n```\n\n**Built-in generic fallback card.** The hot-path selector (see The Hot Path / UI-Regen Agent) is a deterministic lookup: highest `match_score` in `component_usage_patterns` for the result's `result_type`, no LLM. The hot-path server is the one other writer to that DB, confined to mechanical render/usage state on each dispatch: `card_cache` rows and the usage-stat columns (`components.usage_count`/`last_used`, `component_usage_patterns.sample_count`/`last_matched`) — bookkeeping, never templates or definitions. Background-analysis auto-applies are not a third artifact writer: they execute through the self-modification agent's write path, inheriting its scope, freeze/kill-switch behavior, and git-commit rollback (see Background Analysis Bead). Neither agent writes the other's artifacts\n1083\t- All artifact changes go through diff-review before application\n1084\t- Rollback available for any artifact via git history (see Self-Modification Agent safety model)\n1085\t\n1086\t### Action Execution Model\n1087\t\n1088\t**Status: policy in force; executor NOT BUILT ❌ (design-only).** The rules below already bind the one mutation path that exists (escalate-strand beads under Generated-Bead Safety), but no runtime component executes `action` intents: there is no `src/action/` module and nothing interprets the registry's `workflows.steps`. Until the Action strand sketched below ships, `action` intents are **not dispatched** — the server renders a fixed \"action execution isn't built yet\" card offering to requeue the request as a task-profile, which flows through the escalate strand's reviewed-bead path. This is why the Phase 5 demo script excludes `action`.\n1089\t\n1090\tThe `action` intent and the registry's mutating workflow steps never translate into direct kubectl mutation — the read-only proxies could not execute one, and the GitOps rule forbids it even where credentials exist (live edits fight ArgoCD's selfHeal and don't stick). Every permitted action resolves to exactly one of:\n1091\t\n1092\t1. **GitOps mutation** — deploys, restarts, scaling, and config changes execute as edits to manifests in `jedarden/declarative-config` (k8s/ path): commit, push, ArgoCD syncs. Conversational session state lives in a separate SQLite session store.\n- **kubectl proxies** — fetch strand uses existing kubectl proxy access per cluster.\n- **ArgoCD** — fetch strand reads ArgoCD application state via the endpoint mapped to the project's `cluster` in `config/clusters.yaml`: the no-auth read-only proxy `argocd-ro-ardenone-manager-ts.ardenone.com:8444` for ardenone-cluster apps (ArgoCD on ardenone-manager); apexalgo-iad and the other iad-* Spot clusters are managed by rs-manager's ArgoCD, which has no equivalent read-only proxy today (must-fix before the demo — see the Phase 5 known-issues register).\n\nNet-new code: the codebase measures approximately **15,400 lines** of Python. That is a size figure, not a completeness figure — the count includes stubbed paths (ADR-1's Telegram fallback methods, which log a warning and return `False`, count the same as working code). For an honest completeness picture, use the per-phase statuses in Implementation Phases (verified-in-tests vs. verified-live) plus a stub sweep of `src/`, not line or module counts. See the File System Layout section for the module breakdown.\n\n---\n\n## Security Model\n\n- aide-de-camp runs on the Hetzner server; all kubectl access is read-only via the kubectl proxies (same as existing tooling) — cluster mutations happen only as Git operations (see Action Execution Model below)\n- No cluster credentials stored by aide-de-camp; existing proxy infrastructure holds them. aide-de-camp holds no admin kubeconfigs and must never be granted one\n- Write scopes are per-agent and disjoint: the self-modification agent writes only the `prompts/` and `config/` directories within the repo; the UI-regen agent writes only component definitions in the component library — the `components`, `component_versions`, and `component_tags` tables in `data/components.db`, plus the `match_score` mappings it records in `component_usage_patterns`. The hot-path server is the one other writer to that DB, confined to mechanical render/usage state on each dispatch: `card_cache` rows and the usage-stat columns (`components.usage_count`/`last_used`, `component_usage_patterns.sample_count`/`last_matched`) — bookkeeping, never templates or definitions. Background-analysis auto-applies are not a third artifact writer: they execute through the self-modification agent's write path, inheriting its scope, freeze/kill-switch behavior, and git-commit rollback (see Background Analysis Bead). Neither agent writes the other's artifacts\n- All artifact changes go through diff-review before application\n- Rollback available for any artifact via git history (see Self-Modification Agent safety model)\n\n### Action Execution Model\n\n**Status: policy in force; executor NOT BUILT ❌ (design-only).** The rules below already bind the one mutation path that exists (escalate-strand beads under Generated-Bead Safety), but no runtime component executes `action` intents: there is no `src/action/` module and nothing interprets the registry's `workflows.steps`. Until the Action strand sketched below ships, `action` intents are **not dispatched** — the server renders a fixed \"action execution isn't built yet\" card offering to requeue the request as a task-profile, which flows through the escalate strand's reviewed-bead path. This is why the Phase 5 demo script excludes `action`.\n\nThe `action` intent and the registry's mutating workflow steps never translate into direct kubectl mutation — the read-only proxies could not execute one, and the GitOps rule forbids it even where credentials exist (live edits fight ArgoCD's selfHeal and don't stick). Every permitted action resolves to exactly one of:\n\n1. **GitOps mutation** — deploys, restarts, scaling, and config changes execute as edits to manifests in `jedarden/declarative-config` (k8s/ path): commit, push, ArgoCD syncs. A \"restart\" is a manifest-level change (e.g., a rollout-trigger annotation bump) committed to the repo, not `kubectl rollout restart`.\n2. **ArgoCD sync status polling** — after a GitOps commit, aide-de-camp polls the read-only ArgoCD API until the Application reports Synced/Healthy, then reports the outcome. aide-de-camp itself performs no ArgoCD writes; if a sync is genuinely stuck and needs a forced sync or other operator action, that escalates as a reviewed bead (next item), not an API call from aide-de-camp.\n3. **Reviewed escalation bead** — anything not expressible as (1)+(2) becomes an escalate-strand bead, subject to Generated-Bead Safety validation and the user-approval gate, executed by NEEDLE/Claude Code workers under their own credentials.\n\nCredentials per action class:\n\n| Action class | Credential used | Held by |\n|--------------|-----------------|---------|","numLines":30,"startLine":1070,"totalLines":1203}}}

     schema = f.read()\n88\t            conn = self._get_conn()\n89\t            conn.executescript(schema)\n90\t            conn.commit()\n91\t\n92\t    def create_component(\n93\t        self,\n94\t        component_id = f\"comp-{uuid.uuid4().hex[:12]}\"\n113\t        created_at = int(time.time())\n114\t\n115\t        conn = self._get_conn()\n116\t\n117\t        # Create component\n118\t           \"\"\",\n132\t            (component_id, html_template, created_at, change_note)\n133\t        )\n134\t\n135\t        conn.commit()\n136\t\n137\t        return Component(\n138\t def find_best_component(\n171\t        self,\n172\t        result_type: str,\n173\t        result_data: Dict[str, Any]\n174\t    ) -> Optional[Component]:\n175\t        \"\"\"\n176\t        Find the best-fit component for a result.\n177\t\n178\t        Uses semantic matching on component descriptions and\n179\t        historical usage patterns.\n180\t\n181\t        Args:\n182\t            result_type: The type of result (e.g., \"pod-status\")\n183\t            result_data: The structured result data\n184\t\n185\t        Returns:\n186\t            The best matching Component, or None if no good match\n187\t        \"\"\"\n188\t        conn = self._get_conn()\n189\t\n190\t        # First, check usage patterns for this exact result type\n191\t        pattern_row = conn.execute(\n192\t            \"\"\"\n193\t            SELECT component_id, match_score\n194\t            FROM component_usage_patterns\n195\t            WHERE result_type = ?\n196\t            ORDER BY match_score DESC, sample_count DESC\n197\t            LIMIT 1\n198\t            \"\"\",\n199\t            (result_type,)\n200\t        ).fetchone()\n201\t\n202\t        if pattern_row and pattern_row[1] >= 0.7:  # 70% confidence threshold\n203\t            component = self.get_component(pattern_row[0])\n204\t            if component:\n205\t                return component\n206\t\n207\t        # Fallback: semantic search on component descriptions\n208\t        # (In production, this would use embeddings. For now, use simple keyword match)\n209\t        components = conn.execute(\n210\t            \"SELECT id, name, description FROM components WHERE usage_count > 0\"\n211\t        ).fetchall()\n212\t\n213\t        best_component = None\n214\t        best_score = 0.0\n215\t\n216\t        for comp_id, name, description in components:\n217\t            score = self._semantic_score(result_type, name, description)\n218\t            if score > best_score:\n219\t                best_score = score\n220\t                best_component = self.get_component(comp_id)\n221\t\n222\t        # Only return if we have reasonable confidence\n223\t        if best_score >= 0.5:\n224\t            return best_component\n225\t\n226\t        return None\n227\t\n228\t    def select_component_for_result_type(\n229\t        self,\n230\t        result_type: str,\n231\t        match_threshold: float = 0.7,\n232\t    ) -> Optional[Component]:\n233\t        \"\"\"Deterministic hot-path selection — the dispatch card selector.\n234\t\n235\t        Returns the highest-``match_score`` component mapped to ``result_type``\n236\t        in ``component_usage_patterns`` whose score clears ``match_threshold``,\n237\t        or None. No LLM, no semantic fallback, no generation (plan: The Hot Path\n238\t        / UI-Regen Agent — \"highest match_score in component_usage_patterns for\n239\t        the result_type, no LLM call\"). A None return is the signal that the\n240\t        result must fall to the built-in generic fallback card.\n241\t\n242\t        Distinct from :meth:`find_best_component`, which the *async* UI-regen\n243\t        agent uses to steward the library (it may fall back to semantic search\n244\t        and ultimately generate a new component). The hot path must never do\n245\t        either — it only reads the recorded mappings.\n246\t        \"\"\"\n247\t        conn = self._get_conn()\n248\t        pattern_row = conn.execute(\n249\t            \"\"\"\n250\t            SELECT component_id, match_score\n251\t            FROM component_usage_patterns\n252\t            WHERE result_type = ? AND match_score >= ?\n253\t            ORDER BY match_score DESC, sample_count DESC\n254\t            LIMIT 1\n255\t            \"\"\",\n256\t            (result_type, match_threshold),\n257\t        ).fetchone()\n258\t\n259\t        if not pattern_row:\n260\t            return None\n261\t\n262\t        return self.get_component(pattern_row[0])\n263\t\n264\t    def _semantic_score(self, result_type: str, comp_name: str, comp_description: str) -> float:\n265\t        \"\"\"\n266\t        Calculate semantic match score.\n267\t\n268\t        Simple keyword-based scoring. int\n\n\nclass ComponentLibrary:\n    \"\"\"\n    Manages the component library database.\n\n    Provides:\n    - Component storage and versioning\n    - Card caching\n    - Component selection based on result types\n    - Cache invalidation\n    \"\"\"\n\n    LAYOUT_BUCKETS = ['compact', 'normal', 'expanded']\n\n    def __init__(self, db_path: str):\n        \"\"\"\n        Initialize the component library.\n\n        Args:\n            db_path: Path to the SQLite database file\n        \"\"\"\n        self.db_path = Path(db_path)\n        self._conn: Optional[sqlite3.Connection] = None\n        self._ensure_schema()\n\n    def _get_conn(self) -> sqlite3.Connection:\n        \"\"\"Get or create database connection with WAL mode.\"\"\"\n        if self._conn is None:\n            self._conn = sqlite3.connect(self.db_path, check_same_thread=False)\n            self._conn.execute(\"PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL\")\n            self._conn.execute(\"PRAGMA synchronous=NORMAL\")\n        return self._conn\n\n    def _ensure_schema(self):\n        \"\"\"Ensure database schema exists.\"\"\"\n        schema_path = Path(__file__).parent.parent.parent / 'data' / 'schema.sql'\n        if schema_path.exists():\n            with open(schema_path) as f:\n                schema = f.read()\n            conn = self._get_conn()\n            conn.executescript(schema)\n            conn.commit()\n\n    def create_component(\n        self,\n        name: str,\n        description: str,\n        html_template: str,\n        change_note: str = \"Initial version\"\n    ) -> Component:\n        \"\"\"\n        Create a new component.\n\n        Args:\n            name: Component name (e.g., \"pod-status\")\n            description: What result types this handles\n            html_template: The HTML/CSS template\n            change_note: Reason for this version\n\n        Returns:\n            The created Component\n        \"\"\"\n        import uuid\n        component_id = f\"comp-{uuid.uuid4().hex[:12]}\"\n        created_at = int(time.time())\n\n        conn = self._get_conn()\n\n        # Create component\n        conn.execute(\n            \"\"\"\n            INSERT INTO components (id, name, description, html_template, version, created_at, usage_count)\n            VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, 1, ?, 0)\n            \"\"\",\n            (component_id, name, description, html_template, created_at)\n        )\n\n        # Create version history\n        conn.execute(\n            \"\"\"\n            INSERT INTO component_versions (component_id, version, html_template, created_at, change_note)\n            VALUES (?, 1, ?, ?, ?)\n            \"\"\",\n            (component_id, html_template, created_at, change_note)\n        )\n\n        conn.commit()\n\n        return Component(\n            id=component_id,\n            name=name,\n            description=description,\n            html_template=html_template,\n            version=1,\n            created_at=created_at,\n            last_used=None,\n            usage_count=0\n        )\n\n    def get_component(self, component_id: str) -> Optional[Component]:\n        \"\"\"Get a component by ID.\"\"\"\n        conn = self._get_conn()\n        row = conn.execute(\n            \"SELECT id, name, description, html_template, version, created_at, last_used, usage_count \"\n            \"FROM components WHERE id = ?\",\n            (component_id,)\n        ).fetchone()\n\n        if row:\n            return Component(\n                id=row[0],\n                name=row[1],\n                description=row[2],\n                html_template=row[3],\n                version=row[4],\n                created_at=row[5],\n                last_used=row[6],\n                usage_count=row[7]\n            )\n        return None\n\n    def find_best_component(\n        self,\n        result_type: str,\n        result_data: Dict[str, Any]\n    ) -> Optional[Component]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Find the best-fit component for a result.\n\n        Uses semantic matching on component descriptions and\n        historical usage patterns.\n\n        Args:\n            result_type: The type of result (e.g., \"pod-status\")\n            result_data: The structured result data\n\n        Returns:\n            The best matching Component, or None if no good match\n        \"\"\"\n        conn = self._get_conn()\n\n        # First, check usage patterns for this exact result type\n        pattern_row = conn.execute(\n            \"\"\"\n            SELECT component_id, match_score\n            FROM component_usage_patterns\n            WHERE result_type = ?\n            ORDER BY match_score DESC, sample_count DESC\n            LIMIT 1\n            \"\"\",\n            (result_type,)\n        ).fetchone()\n\n        if pattern_row and pattern_row[1] >= 0.7:  # 70% confidence threshold\n            component = self.get_component(pattern_row[0])\n            if component:\n                return component\n\n        # Fallback: semantic search on component descriptions\n        # (In production, this would use embeddings. For now, use simple keyword match)\n        components = conn.execute(\n            \"SELECT id, name, description FROM components WHERE usage_count > 0\"\n        ).fetchall()\n\n        best_component = None\n        best_score = 0.0\n\n        for comp_id, name, description in components:\n            score = self._semantic_score(result_type, name, description)\n            if score > best_score:\n                best_score = score\n                best_component = self.get_component(comp_id)\n\n        # Only return if we have reasonable confidence\n        if best_score >= 0.5:\n            return best_component\n\n        return None\n\n    def select_component_for_result_type(\n        self,\n        result_type: str,\n        match_threshold: float = 0.7,\n    ) -> Optional[Component]:\n        \"\"\"Deterministic hot-path selection — the dispatch card selector.\n\n        Returns the highest-``match_score`` component mapped to ``result_type``\n        in ``component_usage_patterns`` whose score clears ``match_threshold``,\n        or None. No LLM, no semantic fallback, no generation (plan: The Hot Path\n        / UI-Regen Agent — \"highest match_score in component_usage_patterns for\n        the result_type, no LLM call\"). A None return is the signal that the\n        result must fall to the built-in generic fallback card.\n\n        Distinct from :meth:`find_best_component`, which the *async* UI-regen\n        agent uses to steward the library (it may fall back to semantic search\n        and ultimately generate a new component). The hot path must never do\n        either — it only reads the recorded mappings.\n        \"\"\"\n        conn = self._get_conn()\n        pattern_row = conn.execute(\n            \"\"\"\n            SELECT component_id, match_score\n            FROM component_usage_patterns\n            WHERE result_type = ? AND match_score >= ?\n            ORDER BY match_score DESC, sample_count DESC\n            LIMIT 1\n            \"\"\",\n            (result_type, match_threshold),\n        ).fetchone()\n\n        if not pattern_row:\n            return None\n\n        return self.get_component(pattern_row[0])\n\n    def _semantic_score(self, result_type: str, comp_name: str, comp_description: str) -> float:\n        \"\"\"\n        Calculate semantic match score.\n\n        Simple keyword-based scoring. In production, use embeddings.\n        \"\"\"\n        result_type_lower = result_type.lower()\n        comp_name_lower = comp_name.lower()\n        comp_desc_lower = comp_description.lower()\n\n        score = 0.0\n\n        # Exact name match\n        if result_type_lower == comp_name_lower:\n            return 1.0\n\n        # Name contains result type\n        if result_type_lower in comp_name_lower:\n            score += 0.7\n\n        # Description contains keywords from result type\n        result_keywords = result_type_lower.replace('-', ' ').split()\n        for keyword in result_keywords:\n            if keyword in comp_desc_lower:\n                score += 0.1\n\n        return min(score, 1.0)\n\n    def update_component(\n        self,\n        component_id: str,\n        html_template: str,\n        change_note: str\n    ) -> Optional[Component]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Update a component to a new version.\n\n        Invalidates all cached cards using this component.\n\n        Args:\n            component_id: The component to update\n            html_template: The new template\n            change_note: Why this change was made\n\n        Returns:\n            The updated Component, or None if not found\n        \"\"\"\n        conn = self._get_conn()\n\n        # Get current version\n        row = conn.execute(\n            \"SELECT version FROM components WHERE id = ?\",\n            (component_id,)\n        ).fetchone()\n\n        if not row:\n            return None\n\n        new_version = row[0] + 1\n        now = int(time.time())\n\n        # Update component\n        conn.execute(\n            \"\"\"\n            UPDATE components\n            SET html_template = ?, version = ?, last_used = ?\n            WHERE id = ?\n            \"\"\",\n            (html_template, new_version, now, component_id)\n        )\n\n        # Add version history\n        conn.execute(\n            \"\"\"\n            INSERT INTO component_versions (component_id, version, html_template, created_at, change_note)\n            VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\n            \"\"\",\n            (component_id, new_version, html_template, now, change_note)\n        )\n\n        # Invalidate cached cards\n        conn.execute(\n            \"DELETE FROM card_cache WHERE component_id = ?\",\n            (component_id,)\n        )\n\n        conn.commit()\n\n        return self.get_component(component_id)\n\n    def cache_card(\n        self,\n        result_id: str,\n        component_id: str,\n        component_version: int,\n        layout_bucket: str,\n        rendered_html: str\n    ):\n        \"\"\"\n        Cache a rendered card.\n\n        Args:\n            result_id: The result this card renders\n            component_id: The component used\n            component_version: The component version\n            layout_bucket: The layout bucket\n            rendered_html: The rendered HTML\n        \"\"\"\n        if layout_bucket not in self.LAYOUT_BUCKETS:\n            raise ValueError(f\"Invalid layout bucket: {layout_bucket}\")\n\n        conn = self._get_conn()\n        conn.execute(\n            \"\"\"\n            INSERT OR REPLACE INTO card_cache\n            (result_id, component_id, component_version, layout_bucket, rendered_html, created_at)\n            VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\n            \"\"\",\n            (result_id, component_id, component_version, layout_bucket, rendered_html, int(time.time()))\n        )\n        conn.commit()\n\n        # Update component usage stats\n        conn.execute(\n            \"\"\"\n            UPDATE components\n            SET usage_count = usage_count + 1, last_used = ?\n            WHERE id = ?\n            \"\"\",\n            (int(time.time()), component_id)\n        )\n        conn.commit()\n\n    def get_cached_card(\n        self,\n        result_id: str,\n        component_id: str,\n        layout_bucket: str\n    ) -> Optional[CachedCard]:\n        \"\"\"Get a cached card if it exists and is up-to-date.\"\"\"\n        conn = self._get_conn()\n        row = conn.execute(\n            \"\"\"\n            SELECT result_id, component_id, component_version, layout_bucket, rendered_html, created_at\n            FROM card_cache\n            WHERE result_id = ? AND component_id = ? AND layout_bucket = ?\n            \"\"\",\n            (result_id, component_id, layout_bucket)\n        ).fetchone()\n\n        if row:\n            return CachedCard(\n                result_id=row[0],\n                component_id=row[1],\n                component_version=row[2],\n                layout_bucket=row[3],\n                rendered_html=row[4],\n                created_at=row[5]\n            )\n        return None\n\n    def invalidate_result(self, result_id: str):\n        \"\"\"Invalidate all cached cards for a result.\"\"\"\n        conn = self._get_conn()\n        conn.execute(\n            \"DELETE FROM card_cache WHERE result_id = ?\",\n            (result_id,)\n        )\n        conn.commit()\n\n    def record_usage_pattern(\n        self,\n        component_id: str,\n        result_type: str,\n        match_score: float\n    ):\n        \"\"\"\n        Record a component usage pattern for future matching.\n\n        Args:\n            component_id: The component that was used\n            result_type: The type of result it rendered\n            match_score: How well it matched (0-1)\n        \"\"\"\n        conn = self._get_conn()\n        now = int(time.time())\n\n        conn.execute(\n            \"\"\"\n            INSERT INTO component_usage_patterns (component_id, result_type, match_score, sample_count, last_matched)\n            VALUES (?, ?, ?, 1, ?)\n            ON CONFLICT(component_id, result_type)\n            DO UPDATE SET\n                match_score = (match_score * sample_count + ?) / (sample_count + 1),\n                sample_count = sample_count + 1,\n                last_matched = ?\n            \"\"\",\n            (component_id, result_type, match_score, now, match_score, now)\n        )\n        conn.commit()\n\n    def list_components(self, limit: int = 50) -> List[Component]:\n        \"\"\"List all components, ordered by usage.\"\"\"\n        conn = self._get_conn()\n        rows = conn.execute(\n            \"\"\"\n            SELECT id, name, description, html_template, version, created_at, last_used, usage_count\n            FROM components\n            ORDER BY usage_count DESC, last_used DESC\n            LIMIT ?\n            \"\"\",\n            (limit,)\n        ).fetchall()\n\n        return [\n            Component(\n                id=row[0],\n                name=row[1],\n                description=row[2],\n                html_template=row[3],\n                version=row[4],\n                created_at=row[5],\n                last_used=row[6],\n                usage_count=row[7]\n            )\n            for row in rows\n        ]\n\n    def get_component_history(self, component_id: str) -> List[ComponentVersion]:\n        \"\"\"Get version history for a component.\"\"\"\n        conn = self._get_conn()\n        rows = conn.execute(\n            \"\"\"\n            SELECT component_id, version, html_template, created_at, change_note\n            FROM component_versions\n            WHERE component_id = ?\n            ORDER BY version DESC\n            \"\"\",\n            (component_id,)\n        ).fetchone()\n\n        return [\n            ComponentVersion(\n                component_id=row[0],\n                version=row[1],\n                html_template=row[2],\n                created_at=row[3],\n                change_note=row[4]\n            )\n            for row in rows\n        ]\n\n    def rollback_component(self, component_id: str, to_version: int) -> Optional[Component]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Rollback a component to a previous version.\n\n        Args:\n            component_id: The component to rollback\n            to_version: The version to rollback to\n\n        Returns:\n            The rolled-back Component, or None if not found\n        \"\"\"\n        conn = self._get_conn()\n\n        # Get the target version\n        row = conn.execute(\n            \"\"\"\n            SELECT html_template FROM component_versions\n            WHERE component_id = ? AND version = ?\n            \"\"\",\n            (component_id, to_version)\n        ).fetchone()\n\n        if not row:\n            return None\n\n        html_template = row[0]\n\n        # Rollback by updating with the old template as a new version\n        return self.update_component(\n            component_id,\n            html_template,\n            f\"Rollback to version {to_version}\"\n        )\n\n    def close(self):\n        \"\"\"Close the database connection.\"\"\"\n        if self._conn:\n            self._conn.close()\n            self._conn = None\n\n\n# Singleton instance for the application\n_library_instance: Optional[ComponentLibrary] = None\n\n\ndef get_library(db_path: str = '/home/coding/aide-de-camp/data/components.db') -> ComponentLibrary:\n    \"\"\"Get or create the component library singleton.\"\"\"\n    global _library_instance\n    if _library_instance is None:\n        _library_instance = ComponentLibrary(db_path)\n    return _library_instance\n","numLines":567,"startLine":1,"totalLines":567}}}

in\n``component_usage_patterns`` for the result's ``result_type``, no LLM call —\nfills that component's template with the result data per layout bucket, writes\nthe rendered HTML to ``card_cache``, It NEVER writes component definitions\n(``components`` rows, ``component_versions``, ``component_tags``) — that is the\nUI-regen agent's job alone, so the two never race on a component's identity.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport html\nimport re\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass\nfrom logging import getLogger\nfrom typing import Any, Optional\n\nfrom ..components.library import Component, ComponentLibrary, get_library\n\nlogger = getLogger(__name__)\n\n# A component_usage_patterns row must clear this to be selected (plan: \"highest\n# match_score ... no LLM\"; below this the result falls to the built-in fallback).\nDEFAULT_MATCH_THRESHOLD = 0.7\n\n# The hot path renders one card per intent thread at a single layout bucket.\nDEFAULT_LAYOUT_BUCKET = \"normal\"\n\n# Flat ``{{field.path}}`` token (no loops/conditionals) — the same substitution\n# contract the UI-regen generator is told to emit (see ui_regen.py).\n_PLACEHOLDER = re.compile(r\"\\{\\{([^}]+)\\}\\}\")\n\n\ndef derive_result_type(\n    intent_type: Optional[str],\n    project_slug: Optional[str],\n    lookup_kind: Optional[str] = None,\n) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Derive the deterministic card-selector key written to ``results.result_type``.\n\n    - intent-derived: ``\"{intent_type}:{project_slug}\"`` — one per intent thread\n      (the aggregated thread card), never per fetch source.\n    - lookup threads insert the router's ``lookup_kind``:\n      ``\"lookup:{lookup_kind}:{project_slug}\"`` (e.g. ``lookup:logs:ibkr-mcp`` vs\n      ``lookup:config:ibkr-mcp`` — distinct keys, distinct cards).\n    - monitoring-originated rows: ``\"monitoring:{project_slug}\"``.\n\n    A missing project slug collapses to ``\"general\"`` so the key is always a\n    non-empty, colon-segmented string the selector can index on.\n    \"\"\"\n    slug = project_slug or \"general\"\n    itype = intent_type or \"status\"\n    if itype == \"monitoring\":\n        return f\"monitoring:{slug}\"\n    if itype == \"lookup\" and lookup_kind:\n        return f\"lookup:{lookup_kind}:{slug}\"\n    return f\"{itype}:{slug}\"\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass RenderOutcome:\n    \"\"\"Result of a hot-path render: either a real component render, or a fallback flag.\n\n    ``rendered_html`` is None exactly when no component matched (the client must\n    render the built-in generic fallback card). ``card_fallback`` is the boolean\n    mirror persisted onto the result row so loadTopics()/the SSE consumer both\n    see the same decision without re-running the selector.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    rendered_html: Optional[str]\n    component_id: Optional[str]\n    card_fallback: bool\n    layout_bucket: str\n\n\nclass HotPathRenderer:\n    \"\"\"Deterministic server-side card selector — never on the LLM path.\n\n    Selection is a single ``component_usage_patterns`` lookup on\n    ``result_type``; on a match it fills the template (escaping every\n    interpolated value — the render-path escaping boundary), writes\n    ``card_cache``, and bumps the usage stats. On no match it returns a\n    fallback outcome and writes nothing to the component DB.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        library: Optional[ComponentLibrary] = None,\n        match_threshold: float = DEFAULT_MATCH_THRESHOLD,\n        layout_bucket: str = DEFAULT_LAYOUT_BUCKET,\n    ):\n        self.library = library or get_library()\n        self.match_threshold = match_threshold\n        self.layout_bucket = layout_bucket\n\n    def render(\n        self,\n        result_id: str,\n        result_type: str,\n        result_data: dict[str, Any],\n        summary: Optional[str] = None,\n        layout_bucket: Optional[str] = None,\n    ) -> RenderOutcome:\n        \"\"\"Select + render a card for one result. Deterministic, no LLM.\n\n        On a match: fills the template (escaped), writes card_cache keyed\n        ``(result_id, component_id, layout_bucket)``, and records the usage\n        pattern + usage stats. On no match / below threshold: returns a fallback\n        outcome and writes nothing to the component DB (the client renders the\n        built-in generic fallback card from result.data + summary).\n        \"\"\"\n        bucket = layout_bucket or self.layout_bucket\n\n        component = self.library.select_component_for_result_type(\n            result_type, self.match_threshold\n        )\n        if component is None:\n            logger.debug(\n                \"hot-path fallback: no component matched result_type=%s \"\n                \"(threshold %.2f) for result %s\",\n                result_type,\n                self.match_threshold,\n                result_id,\n            )\n            return RenderOutcome(\n                rendered_html=None,\n                component_id=None,\n                card_fallback=True,\n                layout_bucket=bucket,\n            )\n\n        rendered_html = fill_template(component.html_template, result_data)\n\n        # The server is the sole writer of card_cache + usage stats (write-scope\n        # separation). cache_card bumps components.usage_count/last_used;\n        # record_usage_pattern updates component_usage_patterns (sample_count,\n        # last_matched, running match_score). Neither touches component\n        # definitions — that is the UI-regen agent's job.\n        self.library.cache_card(\n            result_id, component.id, component.version, bucket, rendered_html\n        )\n        # A hot-path match is a confirmed, high-confidence usage — record at 1.0\n        # so reliably-matching components trend toward the top of the selector.\n        self.library.record_usage_pattern(component.id, result_type, match_score=1.0)\n\n        logger.info(\n            \"hot-path rendered result %s with component %s v%s (result_type=%s)\",\n            result_id,\n            component.id,\n            component.version,\n            result_type,\n        )\n        return RenderOutcome(\n            rendered_html=rendered_html,\n            component_id=component.id,\n            card_fallback=False,\n            layout_bucket=bucket,\n        )\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Template fill — the render-path escaping boundary\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\ndef fill_template(template: str, result_data: dict[str, Any]) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Flat dot-path substitution with HTML escaping at fill time.\n\n    Every ``{{field.path}}`` token is replaced with the HTML-escaped value at\n    that dot-path (list indices via numeric segments, e.g. ``{{pods.0.name}}``).\n    Unknown paths resolve to \"\". This is the escaping boundary the plan binds\n    every server-filled card to (UI-Regen Agent → \"Escaping contract\"): a\n    markup-looking log line interpolated here renders as literal text instead of\n    breaking layout or executing in the canvas. No loops or conditionals — the\n    generator is instructed to emit only flat placeholders.\n    \"\"\"\n    if not template:\n        return \"\"\n\n    def replace_value(match: re.Match) -> str:\n        path = match.group(1).strip()\n        value = _get_nested_value(result_data, path)\n        if value is None:\n            return \"\"\n        return html.escape(str(value), quote=False)\n\n    return _PLACEHOLDER.sub(replace_value, template)\n\n\ndef _get_nested_value(data: dict[str, Any], path: str) -> Any:\n    \"\"\"Resolve a dot-path against a dict/list (numeric segments index lists).\"\"\"\n    value: Any = data\n    for key in path.split(\".\"):\n        if isinstance(value, dict):\n            value = value.get(key)\n        elif isinstance(value, list) and key.isdigit() and int(key) < len(value):\n            value = value[int(key)]\n        else:\n            return None\n    return value\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Singleton\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n_renderer: Optional[HotPathRenderer] = None\n\n\ndef get_renderer(\n    library: Optional[ComponentLibrary] = None,\n    reset: bool = False,\n) -> HotPathRenderer:\n    \"\"\"Get or create the process-wide hot-path renderer singleton.\n\n    Pass ``library=`` (and ``reset=True``) to rebind it — how tests point the\n    renderer at an isolated component DB without touching the production\n    ``data/components.db`` singleton.\n    \"\"\"\n    global _renderer\n    if _renderer is None or reset or library is not None:\n        _renderer = HotPathRenderer(library=library)\n    return _renderer\n","numLines":231,"startLine":1,"totalLines":231}}}

self.prompt_path = prompt_path or ROUTER_PROMPT_PATH\n95\t        self._zai_client = None\n96\t        self._reload_manager = None\n97\t\n98\t    async def _get_zai_client(self):\n99\t        \"\"\"Get or create ZAI client.\"\"\"\n100\t        if self._zai_client is None:\n101\t            self._zai_client = get_zai_client()\n102\t        return self._zai_client\n103\t\n104\t    async def _get_store(self):\n105\t        \"\"\"Get or create session store.\"\"\"\n106\t        if self.store is None:\n107\t            self.store = get_store()\n108\t        return self.store\n109\t\n110\t    def _get_reload_manager(self):\n111\t        \"\"\"Get or create the hot-reload manager (lazy singleton).\"\"\"\n112\t        if self._reload_manager is None:\n113\t            self._reload_manager = get_reload_manager()\n114\t        return self._reload_manager\n115\t\n116\t    def _load_router_prompt(self) -> str:\n117\t        \"\"\"Load the router segmentation prompt from disk (hot-reload, per call).\"\"\"\n118\t        try:\n119\t            return self.prompt_path.read_text()\n120\t        except Exception as e:\n121\t            logger.error(f\"Failed to load router prompt from {self.prompt_path}: {e}\")\n122\t            return _ROUTER_PROMPT_FALLBACK\n123\t\n124\t    def _load_urgency_prompt(self) -> str:\n125\t        \"\"\"\n126\t        Load the urgency classification prompt from disk via the hot-reload manager.\n127\t\n128\t        prompts/urgency.md is a separately hot-reloadable artifact (registered in\n129\t        src/components/hot_reload.py). Splicing it here keeps urgency guidance\n130\t        editable independently of the segmentation prompt. Returns \"\" on failure\n131\t        so the router still functions without urgency guidance.\n132\t        \"\"\"\n133\t        try:\n134\t            return self._get_reload_manager().get_prompt(\"urgency\")\n135\t        except Exception as e:\n136\t            logger.warning(f\"Failed to load urgency prompt: {e}\")\n137\t            return \"\"\n138\t\n139\t    def _build_system_prompt(self) -> str:\n140\t        \"\"\"Build the full system prompt: segmentation prompt + urgency rules.\"\"\"\n141\t        system_prompt = self._load_router_prompt()\n142\t        urgency_prompt = self._load_urgency_prompt()\n143\t        if urgency_prompt:\n144\t            system_prompt = (\n145\t                f\"{system_prompt}\\n\\n## Urgency Classification Rules\\n\\n{urgency_prompt}\"\n146\t            )\n147\t        return system_prompt\n148\t\n149\t    async def classify_utterance(\n150\t        self,\n151\t        utterance: str,\n152\t        session_id: str,\n153\t    ) -> list[IntentClassification]:\n154\t        \"\"\"\n155\t        Classify an utterance into intents.\n156\t\n157\t        Uses LLM to segment and classify the utterance.\n158\t\n159\t        Args:\n160\t            utterance: The user utterance\n161\t            session_id: Session ID for context\n162\t\n163\t        Returns:\n164\t            List of IntentClassification objects\n165\t        \"\"\"\n166\t        client = await self._get_zai_client()\n167\t\n168\t        # Build user message with session context if available\n169\t        store = await self._get_store()\n170\t        session_context = \"\"\n171\t        try:\n172\t            session = await store.get_session(session_id)\n173\t            if session:\n174\t                # Get recent intents for context\n175\t                recent_intents = await store.get_recent_intents(session_id, limit=5)\n176\t                if recent_intents:\n177\t                    session_context = \"\\n\\nRecent intents in this session:\\n\"\n178\t                    for intent in recent_intents:\n179\t                        session_context += f\"- {intent.get('utterance', '')} ({intent.get('intent_type', 'unknown')})\\n\"\n180\t        except Exception as e:\n181\t            logger.warning(f\"Failed to get session context: {e}\")\n182\t\n183\t        user_message = f\"Classify this utterance:\\n\\n{utterance}{session_context}\"\n184\t\n185\t        logger.info(f\"Classifying utterance for session {session_id}\")\n186\t\n187\t        # Build system prompt per call from prompts/router.md (+ urgency rules),\n188\t        # so edits to either prompt take effect without a server restart.\n189\t        system_prompt = self._build_system_prompt()\n190\t\n191\t        try:\n192\t            response = await client.call_simple(\n193\t                system_prompt=system_prompt,\n194\t                user_message=user_message,\n195\t                model=ModelClass.SONNET.value,\n196\t                max_tokens=2048,\n197\t                temperature=0.3,  # Lower temperature for consistent classification\n198\t            )\n199\t\n200\t            # Strip markdown code fences if present (ZAI proxy wraps in ```json...```)\n201\t            raw = response.strip()\n202\t            if raw.startswith(\"```\"):\n203\t                raw = raw.split(\"\\n\", 1)[-1]\n204\t                raw = raw.rsplit(\"```\", 1)[0].strip()\n205\t\n206\t            # Parse JSON response\n207\t            intents_data = json.loads(raw)\n208\t            classifications = []\n209\t\n210\t            for intent_data in intents_data:\n211\t                # Map string to IntentType enum\n212\t                intent_type_str = intent_data.get(\"intent_type\", \"status\")\n213\t                try:\n214\t                    intent_type = IntentType(intent_type_str)\n215\t                except ValueError:\n216\t                    logger.warning(f\"Unknown intent type: {intent_type_str}\")\n217\t                    intent_type = IntentType.STATUS\n218\t\n219\t                classification = IntentClassification(\n220\t                    intent_type=intent_type,\n221\t                    project_slug=intent_data.get(\"project_slug\"),\n222\t                    confidence=float(intent_data.get(\"confidence\", 0.8)),\n223\t                    utterance_fragment=intent_data.get(\"utterance_fragment\", utterance),\n224\t                    reasoning=intent_data.get(\"reasoning\", \"\"),\n225\t                    urgency=intent_data.get(\"urgency\", \"normal\"),\n226\t                )\n227\t                classifications.append(classification)\n228\t\n229\t            logger.info(f\"Classified {len(classifications)} intents from utterance\")\n230\t            return classifications\n231\t\n232\t        except json.JSONDecodeError as e:\n233\t            logger.error(f\"Failed to parse router response as JSON: {e}\")\n234\t            # Fallback: return single status intent\n235\t            return [\n236\t                IntentClassification(\n237\t                    intent_type=IntentType.STATUS,\n238\t                    utterance_fragment=utterance,\n239\t                    confidence=0.5,\n240\t                    reasoning=\"Classification failed, defaulting to status\",\n241\t                )\n242\t            ]\n243\t        except Exception as e:\n244\t            logger.error(f\"Classification failed: {e}\")\n245\t            raise\n246\t\n247\t    async def route_utterance(\n248\t        self,\n249\t        utterance: str,\n250\t        utterance_id: str,\n251\t        session_id: str,\n252\t    ) -> list[RoutedIntent]:\n253\t        \"\"\"\n254\t        Route an utterance to appropriate strands.\n255\t\n256\t        Classifies the utterance and routes each intent to the correct strand.\n257\t\n258\t        Args:\n259\t            utterance: The user utterance\n260\t            utterance_id: Unique ID for this utterance\n261\t            session_id: Session ID\n262\t\n263\t        Returns:\n264\t            List of RoutedIntent objects\n265\t        \"\"\"\n266\t        # Classify the utterance (one LLM call per utterance — the router stage\n267\t        # is shared across every intent thread it produces, so its duration is\n268\t        # measured once here and stamped onto each RoutedIntent).\n269\t        classify_start = time.monotonic()\n270\t        classifications = await self.classify_utterance(utterance, session_id)\n271\t        router_ms = int((time.monotonic() - classify_start) * 1000)\n272\t\n273\t        routed_intents = []\n274\t        for classification in classifications:\n275\t            # Create intent ID\n276\t            intent_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n277\t\n278\t            routed_intent = RoutedIntent(\n279\t                intent_id=intent_id,\n280\t                classification=classification,\n281\t                session_id=session_id,\n282\t                utterance=classification.utterance_fragment,\n283\t                router_ms=router_ms,\n284\t            )\n285\t            routed_intents.append(routed_intent)\n286\t\n287\t        return routed_intents\n288\t\n289\t    async def process_intent(\n290\t        self,\n291\t        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n292\t    ) -> dict:\n293\t        \"\"\"\n294\t        Process a routed intent by dispatching to the appropriate strand.\n295\t\n296\t        Args:\n297\t            routed_intent: The routed intent to process\n298\t\n299\t        Returns:\n300\t            Result dictionary with status and data\n301\t        \"\"\"\n302\t        classification = routed_intent.classification\n303\t\n304\t        # Capture per-stage timings for this dispatch and persist one\n305\t        # dispatch_timings row (see Latency Budget & Instrumentation). Each\n306\t        # branch records the stages it measures; router_ms is shared across\n307\t        # the utterance's threads and was stamped on the RoutedIntent. Capture\n308\t        # is non-fatal: a persistence failure logs and moves on, never breaking\n309\t        # the dispatch itself.\n310\t        timings = DispatchTimings()\n311\t        timings.record(\"router_ms\", routed_intent.router_ms)\n312\t\n313\t        try:\n314\t            # For task-profile intents, escalate to bead\n315\t            if classification.intent_type == IntentType.TASK_PROFILE:\n316\t                result = await self._escalate_to_bead(routed_intent, timings)\n317\t            else:\n318\t                # For other intents, fetch then synthesize\n319\t                result = await self._fetch_and_synthesize(routed_intent, timings)\n320\t        except Exception:\n321\t            # Persist whatever was captured before the failure, then re-raise.\n322\t            await self._persist_timings(routed_intent.intent_id, timings)\n323\t            raise\n324\t\n325\t        await self._persist_timings(routed_intent.intent_id, timings)\n326\t        return result\n327\t\n328\t    async def _persist_timings(\n329\t        self,\n330\t        intent_id: str,\n331\t        timings: DispatchTimings,\n332\t    ) -> None:\n333\t        \"\"\"Persist the captured dispatch timings. Non-fatal on error.\"\"\"\n334\t        try:\n335\t            store = await self._get_store()\n336\t            await store.record_dispatch_timings(intent_id, **timings.to_fields())\n337\t        except Exception as e:\n338\t            logger.warning(f\"dispatch timings not recorded for {intent_id}: {e}\")\n339\t\n340\t    async def _fetch_and_synthesize(\n341\t        self,\n342\t        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n343\t        timings: DispatchTimings,\n344\t    ) -> dict:\n345\t        \"\"\"\n346\t        Fetch context then synthesize into structured result.\n347\t\n348\t        Args:\n349\t            routed_intent: The routed intent to process\n350\t            timings: Per-stage timing collector; records fetch_first_source_ms,\n351\t                fetch_total_ms, and repo_path = None\n370\t            ssh_target = None\n371\t            host_alias = None\n372\t            registry = get_registry()\n373\t            if registry and classification.project_slug:\n374\t                entry = registry.lookup(classification.project_slug)\n375\t                if entry:\n376\t                    repo_path = str(entry.path)\n377\t                    ssh_target = entry.ssh_target\n378\t                    host_alias = entry.host\n379\t                    location = entry.display_path\n380\t                    logger.info(f\"Resolved '{classification.project_slug}' → {location}\")\n381\t                else:\n382\t                    logger.info(f\"No repo found for slug '{classification.project_slug}'\")\n383\t\n384\t from `cluster` via config/clusters.yaml).\n387\t            # argocd_app defaults to the slug when omitted (see _fetch_argocd_app).\n388\t            project_cfg = (\n389\t                 project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n399\t                    session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n400\t                    repo_path=repo_path,\n401\t   )\n408\t\n409\t            # fetch_first_source_ms = time from fetch start to the first source\n410\t            # resolving (success/fail/timeout — the first progress state on the\n411\t            # pending card). fetch_total_ms = the fetch window close.\n412\t            fetch_start = timings.clock()\n413\t            first_source_at: list[float | None] = [None]\n414\t\n415\t            def           )\n491\t\n492\t            result_id = await store.create_result(\n493\t                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n494\t                topic_id=topic_id,\n495\t IntentType.ACTION: FetchIntentType.ACTION,\n595\t            IntentType.BRAINSTORM: FetchIntentType.BRAINSTORM,\n596\t            IntentType.LOOKUP: FetchIntentType.LOOKUP,\n597\t            IntentType.REMINDER: FetchIntentType.REMINDER,\n598\t            IntentType.SELF_MODIFICATION: FetchIntentType.SELF_MODIFICATION,\n599\t            IntentType.MONITORING_CONFIG: async def _check_fence_for_bead(\n605\t        self,\n606\t        bead_ref: str,\n607\t    ) -> dict | None:\n608\t        \"\"\"\n609\t        Check if a bead has been fenced (has last_refusal_reason or fenced_at set).\n610\t\n611\t        Args:\n612\t            bead_ref: The bead reference to check\n613\t\n614\t        Returns:\n615\t            Fence context dict with   bead_watch = await store.get_bead_watch(bead_ref)\n621\t\n622\t            if not bead_watch:\n623\t                return None\n624\t\n625\t            # Check if bead is fenced (has last_refusal_reason or fenced_at)\n626\t            last_refusal_reason = bead_watch.get(\"last_refusal_reason\")\n627\t            fenced_at = bead_watch.get(\"fenced_at\")\n628\t            refusal_count = bead_watch.get(\"refusal_count\", 0)\n629\t\n630\t            if last_refusal_reason or fenced_at:\n631\t                # Bead is fenced - extract fence context\n632\t                return {\n633\t                    \"bead_id\": bead_ref,\n634\t                    \"refusal_reason\": last_refusal_reason or \"Fenced (no reason provided)\",\n635\t                    \"refusal_count\": refusal_count,\n636\t                    \"fenced_at\": fenced_at,\n637\t                }\n638\t\n639\t            return None\n640\t\n641\t        except Exception as e:\n642\t router can classify.\"\"\"\n    STATUS = \"status\"\n    ACTION = \"action\"\n    BRAINSTORM = \"brainstorm\"\n    LOOKUP = \"lookup\"\n    REMINDER = \"reminder\"\n    SELF_MODIFICATION = \"self-modification\"\n    MONITORING_CONFIG = \"monitoring-config\"\n    TASK_PROFILE = \"task-profile\"  # Escalate to NEEDLE bead\n    CLARIFICATION = \"clarification\"  # Needs user input\n project_slug: str | None = None\n    confidence: float = 1.0\n    utterance_fragment: str = \"\"\n    reasoning: str = \"\"\n    urgency: str = \"normal\"  # critical, high, normal, low\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass RoutedIntent:\n    \"\"\"A routed intent ready for processing.\"\"\"\n    intent_id: str\n    classification: IntentClassification\n    session_id: str\n    utterance: str\n    # router_ms is measured once around classify_utterance() in route_utterance\n    # (one LLM call per utterance) and shared across every intent thread from\n    # that utterance — see Latency Budget & Instrumentation in docs/plan/plan.md.\n    router_ms: int | None = None\n\n\n# Path to the router segmentation prompt. Segment the utterance into \"\n    \"distinct intent threads, classify each, and return ONLY a JSON array of \"\n    \"intent objects.\"\n)\n\n\nclass IntentRouter:\n    \"\"\"\n    Intent Router classifies utterances and routes to appropriate strands.\n\n    For task-profile intents, routes to escalate strand for bead creation.\n    For other intents, routes to fetch + synthesize strands (TODO).\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, store=None, prompt_path: Optional[Path] = None):\n        self.store = store\n        self.prompt_path = prompt_path or ROUTER_PROMPT_PATH\n        self._zai_client = None\n        self._reload_manager = None\n\n    async def _get_zai_client(self):\n        \"\"\"Get or create ZAI client.\"\"\"\n        if self._zai_client is None:\n            self._zai_client = get_zai_client()\n        return self._zai_client\n\n    async def _get_store(self):\n        \"\"\"Get or create session store.\"\"\"\n        if self.store is None:\n            self.store = get_store()\n        return self.store\n\n    def _get_reload_manager(self):\n        \"\"\"Get or create the hot-reload manager (lazy singleton).\"\"\"\n        if self._reload_manager is None:\n            self._reload_manager = get_reload_manager()\n        return self._reload_manager\n\n    def _load_router_prompt(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Load the router segmentation prompt from disk (hot-reload, per call).\"\"\"\n        try:\n            return self.prompt_path.read_text()\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Failed to load router prompt from {self.prompt_path}: {e}\")\n            return _ROUTER_PROMPT_FALLBACK\n\n    def _load_urgency_prompt(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"\n        Load the urgency classification prompt from disk via the hot-reload manager.\n\n        prompts/urgency.md is a separately hot-reloadable artifact (registered in\n        src/components/hot_reload.py). Splicing it here keeps urgency guidance\n        editable independently of the segmentation prompt. Returns \"\" on failure\n        so the router still functions without urgency guidance.\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            return self._get_reload_manager().get_prompt(\"urgency\")\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"Failed to load urgency prompt: {e}\")\n            return \"\"\n\n    def _build_system_prompt(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Build the full system prompt: segmentation prompt + urgency rules.\"\"\"\n        system_prompt = self._load_router_prompt()\n        urgency_prompt = self._load_urgency_prompt()\n        if urgency_prompt:\n            system_prompt = (\n                f\"{system_prompt}\\n\\n## Urgency Classification Rules\\n\\n{urgency_prompt}\"\n            )\n        return system_prompt\n\n    async def classify_utterance(\n        self,\n        utterance: str,\n        session_id: str,\n    ) -> list[IntentClassification]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Classify an utterance into intents.\n\n        Uses LLM to segment and classify the utterance.\n\n        Args:\n            utterance: The user utterance\n            session_id: Session ID for context\n\n        Returns:\n            List of IntentClassification objects\n        \"\"\"\n        client = await self._get_zai_client()\n\n        # Build user message with session context if available\n        store = await self._get_store()\n        session_context = \"\"\n        try:\n            session = await store.get_session(session_id)\n            if session:\n                # Get recent intents for context\n                recent_intents = await store.get_recent_intents(session_id, limit=5)\n                if recent_intents:\n                    session_context = \"\\n\\nRecent intents in this session:\\n\"\n                    for intent in recent_intents:\n                        session_context += f\"- {intent.get('utterance', '')} ({intent.get('intent_type', 'unknown')})\\n\"\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"Failed to get session context: {e}\")\n\n        user_message = f\"Classify this utterance:\\n\\n{utterance}{session_context}\"\n\n        logger.info(f\"Classifying utterance for session {session_id}\")\n\n        # Build system prompt per call from prompts/router.md (+ urgency rules),\n        # so edits to either prompt take effect without a server restart.\n        system_prompt = self._build_system_prompt()\n\n        try:\n            response = await client.call_simple(\n                system_prompt=system_prompt,\n                user_message=user_message,\n                model=ModelClass.SONNET.value,\n                max_tokens=2048,\n                temperature=0.3,  # Lower temperature for consistent classification\n            )\n\n            # Strip markdown code fences if present (ZAI proxy wraps in ```json...```)\n            raw = response.strip()\n            if raw.startswith(\"```\"):\n                raw = raw.split(\"\\n\", 1)[-1]\n                raw = raw.rsplit(\"```\", 1)[0].strip()\n\n            # Parse JSON response\n            intents_data = json.loads(raw)\n            classifications = []\n\n            for intent_data in intents_data:\n                # Map string to IntentType enum\n                intent_type_str = intent_data.get(\"intent_type\", \"status\")\n                try:\n                    intent_type = IntentType(intent_type_str)\n                except ValueError:\n                    logger.warning(f\"Unknown intent type: {intent_type_str}\")\n                    intent_type = IntentType.STATUS\n\n                classification = IntentClassification(\n                    intent_type=intent_type,\n                    project_slug=intent_data.get(\"project_slug\"),\n                    confidence=float(intent_data.get(\"confidence\", 0.8)),\n                    utterance_fragment=intent_data.get(\"utterance_fragment\", utterance),\n                    reasoning=intent_data.get(\"reasoning\", \"\"),\n                    urgency=intent_data.get(\"urgency\", \"normal\"),\n                )\n                classifications.append(classification)\n\n            logger.info(f\"Classified {len(classifications)} intents from utterance\")\n            return classifications\n\n        except json.JSONDecodeError as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Failed to parse router response as JSON: {e}\")\n            # Fallback: return single status intent\n            return [\n                IntentClassification(\n                    intent_type=IntentType.STATUS,\n                    utterance_fragment=utterance,\n                    confidence=0.5,\n                    reasoning=\"Classification failed, defaulting to status\",\n                )\n            ]\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Classification failed: {e}\")\n            raise\n\n    async def route_utterance(\n        self,\n        utterance: str,\n        utterance_id: str,\n        session_id: str,\n    ) -> list[RoutedIntent]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Route an utterance to appropriate strands.\n\n        Classifies the utterance and routes each intent to the correct strand.\n\n        Args:\n            utterance: The user utterance\n            utterance_id: Unique ID for this utterance\n            session_id: Session ID\n\n        Returns:\n            List of RoutedIntent objects\n        \"\"\"\n        # Classify the utterance (one LLM call per utterance — the router stage\n        # is shared across every intent thread it produces, so its duration is\n        # measured once here and stamped onto each RoutedIntent).\n        classify_start = time.monotonic()\n        classifications = await self.classify_utterance(utterance, session_id)\n        router_ms = int((time.monotonic() - classify_start) * 1000)\n\n        routed_intents = []\n        for classification in classifications:\n            # Create intent ID\n            intent_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n\n            routed_intent = RoutedIntent(\n                intent_id=intent_id,\n                classification=classification,\n                session_id=session_id,\n                utterance=classification.utterance_fragment,\n                router_ms=router_ms,\n            )\n            routed_intents.append(routed_intent)\n\n        return routed_intents\n\n    async def process_intent(\n        self,\n        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n    ) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Process a routed intent by dispatching to the appropriate strand.\n\n        Args:\n            routed_intent: The routed intent to process\n\n        Returns:\n            Result dictionary with status and data\n        \"\"\"\n        classification = routed_intent.classification\n\n        # Capture per-stage timings for this dispatch and persist one\n        # dispatch_timings row (see Latency Budget & Instrumentation). Each\n        # branch records the stages it measures; router_ms is shared across\n        # the utterance's threads and was stamped on the RoutedIntent. Capture\n        # is non-fatal: a persistence failure logs and moves on, never breaking\n        # the dispatch itself.\n        timings = DispatchTimings()\n        timings.record(\"router_ms\", routed_intent.router_ms)\n\n        try:\n            # For task-profile intents, escalate to bead\n            if classification.intent_type == IntentType.TASK_PROFILE:\n                result = await self._escalate_to_bead(routed_intent, timings)\n            else:\n                # For other intents, fetch then synthesize\n                result = await self._fetch_and_synthesize(routed_intent, timings)\n        except Exception:\n            # Persist whatever was captured before the failure, then re-raise.\n            await self._persist_timings(routed_intent.intent_id, timings)\n            raise\n\n        await self._persist_timings(routed_intent.intent_id, timings)\n        return result\n\n    async def _persist_timings(\n        self,\n        intent_id: str,\n        timings: DispatchTimings,\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Persist the captured dispatch timings. Non-fatal on error.\"\"\"\n        try:\n            store = await self._get_store()\n            await store.record_dispatch_timings(intent_id, **timings.to_fields())\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"dispatch timings not recorded for {intent_id}: {e}\")\n\n    async def _fetch_and_synthesize(\n        self,\n        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n        timings:
